tool_args.label: 34th Street Transitway (Selected BRT Alternative)
tool_args.raw_text: The BRT Alternative (\"34th Street Transitway\") is the Selected Alternative

payload.description: Selected BRT alternative for 34th Street following alternatives analysis screening. Two-way Transitway aligned along curb for length of 34th Street. Transitway occupies half of street: north half east of Fifth Avenue, south half east of Sixth Avenue, with a new plaza from Fifth to Sixth. General traffic one-way eastbound east of Fifth Avenue, one-way westbound west of Sixth Avenue.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The BRT Alternative ("34th Street Transitway") is the Selected Alternative
Two-way Transitway for length of 34th Street aligned along curb
BRT Alternative – Meets Primary Goals and Objectives

tool_args.label: 34th Street Transitway (Selected BRT Alternative)
tool_args.raw_text: The BRT Alternative (\"34th Street Transitway\") is the Selected Alternative

payload.description: Selected BRT alternative for 34th Street following alternatives analysis screening. Two-way Transitway aligned along curb for length of 34th Street. Transitway occupies half of street, north half east of Fifth Avenue, south half east of Sixth Avenue, with a new plaza from Fifth to Sixth. General Traffic: One-Way Eastbound only east of Fifth Avenue, One-Way Westbound only west of Sixth Avenue.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The BRT Alternative (“34 th Street Transitway”) is the Selected Alternative

• Two-way Transitway for length of 34 th Street aligned along curb • Single side alignment selected based on street width and corridor needs – street is too narrow to place Transitway down center of street, and allow parking on either side of the street. • Transitway occupies half of street, north half east of Fifth Avenue, south half east of Sixth Avenue, with a new plaza from Fifth to Sixth • General Traffic: One-Way Eastbound only east of Fifth Avenue One-Way Westbound only west of Sixth Avenue
• No Build Alternative – Alternatives Analysis Requirement • TSM Alternative – Meets Primary Goals and Objectives • BRT Alternative – Meets Primary Goals and Objectives

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: 34th Street Transitway Corridor
tool_args.raw_text: Two-way Transitway for length of 34th Street aligned along curb

payload.limits: Twelfth Avenue/Javits Center to E 34th Street Ferry Terminal
payload.description: Proposed Transitway Corridor running from Twelfth Avenue/Javits Center to E 34th Street Ferry Terminal. Transitway aligned along curb on 34th Street, occupying half of street.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The map illustrates the proposed Transitway Corridor in New York City, running diagonally from the northwest to the southeast. The route is marked with a red dashed line. Key features include: Stations: BRT Stations are marked with yellow squares, and Express Bus Stations are marked with blue squares. Streets: The corridor passes through or near Twelfth Avenue/Javits Center, Eleventh Avenue, Tenth Avenue, Ninth Avenue, Eighth Avenue, Seventh Avenue, Sixth Avenue/Broadway, Madison/Fifth Avenue, Park Avenue, Third/Lexington Avenue, Second Avenue, and First Avenue. Transit Lines: Specific MTA lines are indicated at various points: A, C, and E at Eighth Avenue; 1, 2, and 3 at Seventh Avenue; B, D, F, V, N, Q, R, and W at Sixth Avenue/Broadway; and 6 at Park Avenue. Terminations: The route begins at Twelfth Avenue/Javits Center and ends at the E 34th Street Ferry Terminal.

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Definition of Bus Rapid Transit (BRT)
tool_args.raw_text: High quality surface transit - faster and more reliable. Physical and operational improvements to bus service: high quality bus lanes, prepayment fare collection, transit signal priority, other service enhancements.

payload.claim_text: High quality surface transit – faster and more reliable. Physical and operational improvements to bus service: high quality bus lanes, prepayment fare collection, transit signal priority, other service enhancements. First implemented on Bx12 route – 20% travel time savings.

evidence_refs.source_quote: • High quality surface transit – faster and more reliable • Physical and operational improvements to bus service: – High quality bus lanes – Prepayment fare collection – Transit signal priority – Other service enhancements • First implemented on Bx12 route – 20% travel time savings

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Failed Alternatives - Primary Screening
tool_args.raw_text: Streetcar, Light Rail Transit, Automated Guideway Transit, and Heavy Rail alternatives failed primary screening

payload.claim_text: Streetcar, Light Rail Transit (LRT), Automated Guideway Transit (AGT), and Heavy Rail alternatives failed primary screening. Key reasons: property acquisition for maintenance/storage, loading/parking restrictions, does not improve express bus operations, construction costs and timeline, visual impacts to streetscape.

evidence_refs.source_quote: • Streetcar: – Acquisition of property required for maintenance/storage facility – Loading/parking along 34 th Street completely restricted • LRT: – Does not improve express bus operations – Acquisition of property required for maintenance/storage facility – Loading/parking along 34 th Street completely restricted • AGT: – Construction costs and timeline do not meet Goals and Objectives – Visual impacts to streetscape – Does not improve express bus operations • Heavy Rail Alternative: – Construction costs and timeline do not meet Goals and Objectives – Does not improve express bus operations

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
